# Table Formatting Examples for MLSysBook

This file demonstrates proper table alignment techniques for technical content.

## Example 1: Numerical Precision Formats

**Good alignment** (content-based):
+----------------------------------------+-----------+-----------------------------+---------------------------------------------+
| Precision Format                       | Bit-Width | Storage Reduction (vs FP32) | Compute Speed (vs FP32)                     |
+:=======================================+==========:+============================:+============================================:+
| FP32 (Single-Precision Floating Point) |    32-bit |               Baseline (1×) |                           Baseline (1×)     |
| FP16 (Half-Precision Floating Point)   |    16-bit |                   2× smaller|              2× faster on FP16-optimized   |
| INT8 (8-bit Integer)                   |     8-bit |                   4× smaller|                      4–8× faster than FP32 |
+----------------------------------------+-----------+-----------------------------+---------------------------------------------+

**Analysis:**
- Column 1: Left-aligned (descriptive text)
- Column 2: Center-aligned (categorical technical terms)
- Column 3: Right-aligned (numerical comparisons)
- Column 4: Right-aligned (performance metrics)

## Example 2: System Requirements

**Good alignment**:
+-------------------+---------------------+-----------------+--------------------------------+
| Technique         | Memory Requirements | Compute Overhead| Typical Use Cases              |
+:==================+:===================:+:===============:+================================+
| Bias-Only Updates |               Minimal|              Low| Simple personalization        |
| Residual Adapters |              Moderate|         Moderate| User-specific mobile tuning   |
| Meta-Learning     |                  High|             High| Fast adaptation across devices |
+-------------------+---------------------+-----------------+--------------------------------+

**Analysis:**
- Column 1: Left-aligned (descriptive names)
- Column 2: Center-aligned (categorical levels)
- Column 3: Center-aligned (categorical levels)
- Column 4: Left-aligned (descriptive use cases)

## Example 3: Performance Benchmarks

**Good alignment**:
+----------------+----------+---------------+----------------+---------------------+
| Model          | Platform | Latency (ms)  | Throughput     | Power Consumption   |
+:===============+==========+==============:+===============:+====================:+
| MobileNet v2   |    CPU   |         25.3  |     39.5 FPS   |               1.2W  |
| MobileNet v2   |    GPU   |          8.7  |    115.0 FPS   |               3.8W  |
| EfficientNet   |    TPU   |          4.2  |    238.1 FPS   |               2.1W  |
+----------------+----------+---------------+----------------+---------------------+

**Analysis:**
- Column 1: Left-aligned (model names)
- Column 2: Center-aligned (platform categories)
- Column 3: Right-aligned (numerical measurements)
- Column 4: Right-aligned (numerical measurements)
- Column 5: Right-aligned (numerical measurements)

## Key Principles Demonstrated

1. **Content drives alignment**: The type of data determines the best alignment
2. **Consistency within columns**: All cells in a column use the same alignment
3. **Visual hierarchy**: Right-aligned numbers create clean comparison lines
4. **Readability first**: Choose alignment that makes content easier to scan

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

### ❌ Poor alignment (everything left-aligned):
+----------------+----------+---------------+----------------+
| Model          | Platform | Latency (ms)  | Throughput     |
+================+==========+===============+================+
| MobileNet v2   | CPU      | 25.3          | 39.5 FPS       |
| MobileNet v2   | GPU      | 8.7           | 115.0 FPS      |
| EfficientNet   | TPU      | 4.2           | 238.1 FPS      |
+----------------+----------+---------------+----------------+

**Problems:**
- Numbers don't align for easy comparison
- Mixed content types treated the same
- Harder to scan values quickly

### ✅ Good alignment (content-based):
The examples above show how proper alignment enhances readability and makes data comparison intuitive.
